purpose: Phase 5 Evidence Strength Ladder and Claim Classifier for the Epstein Command Center. This makes public-facing evidence stronger by showing readers exactly how much weight each record type carries.
boundary: The stronger the claim, the stronger the required evidence. Conviction, plea, court finding, authenticated exhibit, sworn testimony, email record, flight/contact record, settlement, news report, and speculation are not the same evidentiary weight.

levels.name: Conviction / Plea / Court Finding
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state the finding precisely and link to the legal record.
levels.warning: Do not extend the finding beyond the named person, charge, count, court, and date.

levels.name: Authenticated Exhibit / Official Disclosure
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state that the document exists, what it says, and where it came from.
levels.warning: The document proves the record item; interpretation still needs context.

levels.name: Sworn Testimony / Deposition / Congressional Testimony
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state that a person testified, alleged, denied, or described something under a formal process.
levels.warning: Sworn testimony is evidence, not automatic final truth unless adopted by a court or finding.

levels.name: Email / Schedule / Ledger / Contact Record
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state that a communication, scheduling item, payment trail, or contact record exists and classify its network function.
levels.warning: Contact proves contact. It does not automatically prove motive, knowledge, or criminal conduct.

levels.name: Flight / Visitor / Property / Logistics Record
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state a recorded travel, property, visitor, or logistics trail and ask what it connects to.
levels.warning: Presence is not automatic participation. Tie it to dates, context, and other records.

levels.name: Settlement / NDA / Legal Threat / Sealed-Record Lane
levels.claimAllowed: The site may state that a settlement, NDA, sealing fight, or legal-pressure event exists and explain why it affects public knowledge.
levels.warning: Settlement is not automatic admission unless the record says so.

levels.name: Reputable Reporting / Named Source Summary
levels.claimAllowed: The site may summarize what a reputable outlet reported and link to it while seeking the underlying file.
levels.warning: Reporting is not the same as the underlying document. Use it as a route into the record.

levels.name: Open Question / Pattern Signal / Speculation Quarantine
levels.claimAllowed: The site may ask a labelled question if it names the triggering record and what would confirm or falsify the theory.
levels.warning: Never write speculation as fact. Never add a name without a record.

claimClassifier.allowedLanguage: The file names this person or entity in this record context.
The record supports contact, communication, or relationship-frequency analysis.
The record supports a presence/logistics trail that must be cross-checked against date, place, witness, and purpose.
The record supports a money, access, legitimacy, consulting, donation, or reputation-laundering question.
The record supports a silence-management, legal-pressure, or private-resolution question.
The record conflicts with a public statement or creates a credibility-pressure lane.
The site may state the conviction or finding exactly as the court record supports it.
claimClassifier.forbiddenShortcut: One person’s conviction = everyone around them is guilty
